atoms.semantic_claim: `reth-execution-errors` exposes an `async-context` feature that forwards to `alloy-evm/async-context`, allowing downstream feature chains to enable async-only block execution error variants through this crate.

destinations.evidence: reth-execution-errors now forwards async-context into alloy-evm.

atoms.equivalence_argument: The downstream async-context feature re-export is restored on reth-execution-errors.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: In the port repo, add a minimal feature-resolution check that enables `reth-errors/async-context` or `reth-evm/async-context` from a small crate. It should fail today because `crates/errors/Cargo.toml` and `crates/evm/Cargo.toml` do not define those features, and `crates/evm/execution-errors/Cargo.toml` does not forward `alloy-evm/async-context`.
atoms.notes: Feature graph check passed for reth-execution-errors --features async-context.

summary: Atoms in this file have destination evidence or exact coverage after the feature-ordered pass.
notes: Updated during Core async first pass; unresolved atoms in the same feature remain separate and require active-consumer or runtime evidence.
